title: BTS Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk's travel ban controversy emerged on April 20: Korea Times reported an unspecified party sought a US Embassy diplomatic exception to allow Bang to travel for BTS's North America tour. HYBE denied requesting the intervention. BTS prepares to open the ARIRANG World Tour's North America leg at Raymond James Stadium, Tampa, FL on April 25–28 (4 sold-out nights, ~220,000 fans). ARIRANG has held a historic 3 consecutive Billboard 200 No. 1 weeks; Week 4 chart data pending.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: BTS completed the Tokyo Dome 2-night run (April 17–18) with 110,000 total fans — their first Japan concerts in approximately seven years. Post-concert reviews from the Japan Times ('After years away, BTS and its fans reunite at full volume') and Starnews Korea highlight BTS's emotional reaction and ARMY's passion. ARIRANG remains at 3 consecutive Billboard 200 No. 1 weeks (K-pop first). The ARIRANG World Tour advances to North America with the Tampa, FL leg at Raymond James Stadium opening April 25–28.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 18, 2026
summary: BTS completed the Tokyo Dome 2-night run (April 17–18), their first Japan concerts in approximately seven years and the first overseas leg of the Arirang World Tour. Night 1 debuted 'Save Me' and 'Crystal Snow' (first performance since 2018); Night 2 was live-broadcast to 3,500+ cinemas in 75 countries. ARIRANG locked a third consecutive No. 1 on the Billboard 200 (April 18 chart, 124K units) — the first K-pop album in history to achieve this, and the first group album in 13 years. South Korea reported a record 2.06 million foreign visitors in March, partly attributed to the BTS comeback effect.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: BTS performs the first of two sold-out nights at Tokyo Dome on April 17, 2026 (doors 4:30 PM / show 6:30 PM JST) — their first concerts in Japan in approximately seven years.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: South Korea's Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism announced on April 16, 2026 that the country recorded 2.06 million foreign visitors in March 2026 — a record high for the month. HYBE revealed the final performer lineup for the 2026 Weverse Con Festival (scheduled June 6–7, 2026 at KSPO DOME, Seoul) on April 16.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: Six of the seven BTS members departed from Gimpo Airport on April 15, 2026, flying to Tokyo for the Arirang World Tour's first overseas leg at Tokyo Dome.

title: Breaking: HYBE is pursuing legal action to unmask and sue the X user who leaked BTS's 'ARI
summary: HYBE is pursuing legal action to unmask and sue the X user who leaked BTS's 'ARIRANG' before its official release, escalating the label's response to the leak beyond a takedown to active litigation.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Mon Apr 13, 2026
summary: Billboard confirms ARIRANG held No.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 12, 2026
summary: BTS closed the three-night Goyang Stadium opening run on April 12 with the live debut of 'Come Over' — the vinyl-exclusive hidden track produced by Suga. The final show featured traditional Korean production elements including gayageum melodies and Seungmu-inspired choreography, earning strong critical praise. HYBE signed a $100M+ global distribution deal with K Wave Media (KWM) on April 10. The Arirang World Tour's Goyang Night 2 on April 11 was broadcast live to cinemas in 75+ countries, while Billboard confirmed ARIRANG at #1 for a second consecutive week (187K units); the album is now tracking toward an unprecedented third consecutive No. 1 on the April 18-dated chart.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: BTS performs the second of three sold-out Goyang Stadium shows on April 11, 2026 — broadcast live simultaneously to cinemas in 75+ countries, the largest theatrical simulcast of the ARIRANG tour. Billboard Confirms ARIRANG at No. 1 for Second Consecutive Week — 187K Units; K-pop First.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: K Wave Media Ltd.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: BTS officially opened the ARIRANG World Tour on April 9, 2026 at Goyang Stadium, South Korea — their first full headline concert in three and a half years. Despite rain, 40,000+ fans packed the 360-degree in-the-round stage for the opening night of three sold-out Goyang dates. A simultaneous global live cinema viewing streamed to 35+ countries via Fandango. Run BTS! 2.0 premiered April 7 on Bangtan TV YouTube and Weverse, with all seven members reunited for the first time in variety content since 2022. The ARMY presale for Latin America dates opened April 7, with three additional shows added to Lima, Santiago, and Buenos Aires on April 8 due to overwhelming demand. KPIs updated to 4,683 days since debut.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: BTS added three additional concerts to the Latin American leg of the Arirang World Tour on April 8, 2026, scheduling one extra show each in Lima (Peru), Santiago (Chile), and Buenos Aires (Argentina). BTS Goyang Opening-Night Resale Tickets Hit 40× Face Value on Secondary Market.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Run BTS! 2.0 Episode 1 'The Reunion: Back to the Start' premieres on April 7, 2026 at 9PM KST on the official Bangtan TV YouTube channel and Weverse — free to all viewers globally. The ARMY Membership (Global) presale for the Arirang World Tour Latin America dates opened on April 7, 2026 at designated local times, with selected shows continuing on April 8.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: ARIRANG made K-pop history by holding #1 on the Billboard 200 for a second consecutive week (187K units), becoming the first K-pop album ever to achieve back-to-back weeks at the top. The ARIRANG Deluxe Vinyl dropped April 3 with a vinyl-exclusive hidden track 'Come Over,' produced by Suga — sparking fan enthusiasm and debate over digital accessibility. HYBE debunked fabricated streaming data spread online and filed a rare international lawsuit against an online defamer. Visa was announced as the Worldwide Tour Sponsor for BTS WORLD TOUR 'ARIRANG,' covering 82 shows across 23 countries. The Arirang World Tour opens in Goyang, South Korea on April 9.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: ARIRANG Holds #1 on Billboard 200, Spotify Global, YouTube Music, and ARIA Charts.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Following the April 3 fake streaming data controversy, HYBE escalated its response on April 4, filing a rare international lawsuit against an Argentine online defamer.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: BTS releases the ARIRANG Deluxe Vinyl on April 3, 2026, featuring a vinyl-exclusive hidden track 'Come Over' produced by Suga (Min Yoon-gi), with additional credits to RM and J-Hope. On April 3, 2026, a fabricated image spread on social media platform X and online forums claiming that 'SWIM' — ARIRANG's lead single — recorded only 5,198 daily Spotify streams as of April 1.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Billboard officially confirmed ARIRANG debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 with 641,000 equivalent album units (532K pure sales) — BTS's 7th chart-topping album and the biggest US album debut of 2026. 'SWIM' simultaneously topped the Billboard Hot 100 (BTS's 7th #1 single), making BTS the first Korean act to simultaneously lead both charts. 13 of ARIRANG's 14 tracks charted on the Hot 100 simultaneously, a K-pop record, while BTS occupied positions #1–#13 on the Billboard Global 200. 'Run BTS 2.0' was announced on March 30, with the variety show revival premiering April 7 on YouTube and Weverse ahead of the Arirang World Tour launch on April 9.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: Post-ceremony coverage confirms BTS received 5 nominations at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, recognizing their ARIRANG comeback.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: Billboard officially confirms 'Swim' debuted at No. BTS Places Songs at #1–#13 on Billboard Global 200 — Historic Chart Sweep.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Billboard officially confirms ARIRANG debuted at No. BTS releases a teaser video on their official Bangtan TV YouTube channel announcing the return of 'Run BTS,' their beloved variety show that ran from 2015 to 2022 (150+ episodes).

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sun Mar 29, 2026
summary: Following initial post-comeback HYBE stock volatility (which had dropped ~15% on March 23 before rebounding ~3.9%), multiple global financial analysts publish bullish reports.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: BTS's landmark week: 'ARIRANG' (5th studio album, first since 2020) released March 20 to all-time records — 3.98M Hanteo Day 1 sales, 110M Spotify Day 1 streams (K-pop record), and all 14 tracks simultaneously topping Spotify's Global Top 50. The March 21 Gwanghwamun comeback concert drew ~260,000 in-person attendees and 18.4 million Netflix livestream viewers, becoming the largest public concert in South Korean history. BTS appeared on The Tonight Show two consecutive nights (March 25–26), including a surprise performance at the Guggenheim Museum. Netflix also released 'BTS: The Return' documentary on March 27. ARIRANG is tracking at 600K–625K US first-week units, on pace for the biggest US album debut of 2026.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Thu Mar 26, 2026
summary: BTS performs ARIRANG track '2.0' at the Guggenheim Museum in New York City for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon's second consecutive BTS night — a surprise special venue set.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: BTS appears on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon for the first of two consecutive nights.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: ARIRANG lead single 'Swim' holds No.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: BTS performs a free public comeback concert at Gwanghwamun Square in central Seoul — their first full-group performance since October 2022. Netflix Livestream 'BTS: The Comeback Live | Arirang' Draws 18.4M Viewers.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: BTS releases 'ARIRANG,' their 5th full-length studio album and first group release since 'BE' (Nov 2020). ARIRANG accumulates 110 million Spotify streams on its first day — the highest single-day total for any K-pop album in Spotify history and 12th highest globally of all time.
